videos.title: First Dallas, Then Minnesota, Now Colorado? What’s Up With The Big Bad Central? | DLLS Stars Podcast
videos.summary_source_text:
First Dallas, Then Minnesota, Now Colorado? What’s Up With The Big Bad Central? | DLLS Stars Podcast Become a DLLS Diehard and get access to premium content, our Discord channel, discounts on merch, and a free shirt! Sign up here: https://alldlls.com/join-diehard/

The Dallas Stars finished in second place in the Central Division AND second overall in the Western Conference. The Colorado Avalanche, the President’s Trophy winner, finished first and the Minnesota Wild had the third best record, However, they all seem to be suffering the same playoff fate - injuries to key players stalling an otherwise promising Stanley Cup campaign. The Stars crashed out in the first round to the Wild, Minnesota were summarily dispatched by the Avalanche in the second round and now Colorado is down 3-0 in the Western Conference Final to the Vegas Golden Knights. Today, we’ll be joined by Jess Myers, Wild beat writer for the St. Paul Pioneer Press and we will discuss this trend, as well as doing a general post mortem on the Minnesota Wild. Additionally, we will talk about Thomas Harley’s disappointing season and we’ll look at the current state of the Stanley Cup Playoffs.

OpenHuman vs Hermes Agent: Which AI Agent Tool Should You Use?

The video compares OpenHuman and Hermes Agent, two trending AI agent projects, noting OpenHuman’s recent release and 28,000 GitHub stars versus Hermes’ 169,000. It shows how to install OpenHuman as a desktop app on Mac, Windows, or Linux and highlights its non-technical, app-like UI, connections, and built-in sections like an app builder and “coming soon” calls and memory/reflections. The creator questions OpenHuman’s credit system even when using personal models, and demonstrates limitations such as lack of scheduled tools, buggy or unsuccessful deeper tasks like emailing and long-form SEO writing, and limited customization and multi-agent workflows. Hermes is presented as more powerful and autonomous, supporting scheduled tasks and use of free APIs, and the creator recommends building an agent operating system around Hermes for better UI, history, and large task execution. The episode ends by promoting an AI Profit Boardroom offering with tutorials, downloads, prompts, community, and weekly coaching.

videos.title: Texas Rangers Searching For Answers After Getting No-Hit By Houston Astros | DLLS Rangers Podcast
videos.summary_source_text:
Texas Rangers Searching For Answers After Getting No-Hit By Houston Astros | DLLS Rangers Podcast Take advantage of our Playoff Diehard Sale and join our community today! https://alldlls.com/stars-playoff-offer-youtube 

An ALLCITY Network Production

The Texas Rangers’ season hit a new low Monday night. They were no-hit by the rival Houston Astros, who used three pitchers for the third no-no against the Rangers in club history. Tatsuya Imai started the game with two walks, but fixed things quickly. The Rangers didn’t strike out much, but they also didn’t have much hard contact. The question against is how do they fix the offense. Manager Skip Schumaker talked about messaging and game planning. Brandon Nimmo talked about execution. Sounds like a lot of work with less than a full arsenal of players to do it.

videos.title: UK | 26th May, Tuesday - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
UK | 26th May, Tuesday - RIFT Presentation 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

⚠️ Disclaimer: Trading involves risk. Always understand fees, leverage, and market conditions before trading. 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

videos.title: Uniform rumors, 12 predictions for year 1 in the Pac-12 + will we miss the CSU-Air Force rivalry?
videos.summary_source_text:
Uniform rumors, 12 predictions for year 1 in the Pac-12 + will we miss the CSU-Air Force rivalry? Justin Michael begins the show by talking about what he’s hearing about the future of CSU Athletics and Under Amour, as well as why he’d like to see a uniform refresh for the Pac-12 era. Justin then gives a few thoughts on the CSU-Air Force rivalry and whether or not it’s a tradition the fans will truly miss. Finally, Justin gives out 12 bold predictions for Colorado State Football in 2026 and reacts to the predictions sent in on social media.

Build an Agent Operating System: Hermes vs OpenClaw, Local Models, Video Agents & Obsidian Memory

The video explains how to build an “agent operating system” that centralizes AI agents, tools, outputs, and memory in one dashboard so work stays organized and improves over time. It answers common builder questions: Hermes and OpenClaw don’t replace each other, but Hermes is recommended first because it’s smoother, less buggy, and includes goal-based workflows with a Kanban board; OpenClaw can be added later. For daily work, use the operating system (with agents reading and logging to an Obsidian vault), while Claude Code is better for deeply technical debugging or large system improvements. It shows how to reduce token costs by running local models via Ollama or LM Studio and highlights a free, self-run video agent using Hyperframe CLI. Finally, it covers tying everything together with an Obsidian memory system (optionally fed by OMI) so agents retain context and sound like you.

videos.title: The Colorado Avalanche Seasons Ends in a Fashion Nobody Saw Coming...Getting Swept
videos.summary_source_text:
The Colorado Avalanche Seasons Ends in a Fashion Nobody Saw Coming...Getting Swept Colorado Avalanche’s season ends by a stunning sweep by the Vegas Golden Knights. Can this Cup-caliber roster recover from such a shocking playoff collapse?

Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson break down how Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, and the Avalanche’s high-powered offense fell flat despite outshooting opponents and dominating regular season stats. Discussion highlights include the team’s struggles to generate quality chances, Vegas’ relentless defensive pressure, and lingering questions about coaching—will Jared Bednar remain behind the bench after a decade at the helm? The duo also spotlights upcoming offseason decisions for GM Chris MacFarland, the future of core players, and whether a rebuild—or a simple retool—is on the horizon.

Don’t miss this candid postmortem of the Colorado Avalanche’s abrupt playoff exit, with insights on what went wrong and what’s next as the franchise faces a pivotal summer.

videos.title: Hindi | 26th May, Tuesday - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
Hindi | 26th May, Tuesday - RIFT Presentation 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

⚠️ Disclaimer: Trading involves risk. Always understand fees, leverage, and market conditions before trading. 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

Hermes Agent has officially added Qwen 3.7 Max, letting users run Hermes on this agentic model via OpenRouter, where Hermes is currently the top app using it, and take advantage of a 1M-token context window. The script explains what Qwen 3.7 Max is (from Alibaba’s Qwen team), why it’s strong for long-horizon autonomous work (including a documented 35-hour optimization run), and how to enable it in Hermes by running a terminal update, selecting OpenRouter, adding an API key, and choosing the model; it also notes a free alternative using OwlAlpha with Hermes H1. It discusses benchmark comparisons versus Opus 4.6, highlights improved tool use, coding/terminal performance, reasoning, and long-context recall, and demonstrates using Hermes goal mode plus Obsidian integration to pull in large note sets, generate automation ideas, and write back into the vault. videos.title: Rapids Stumble Into World Cup Break as USMNT Announces Final Roster with Heath Pearce
videos.summary_source_text:
Rapids Stumble Into World Cup Break as USMNT Announces Final Roster with Heath Pearce The Colorado Rapids are stumbling into the MLS World Cup break following frustrating consecutive losses to RSL and FC Dallas. Staying alive in the U.S. Open Cup we give our biggest takeaways. 
On today's episode of the DNVR Rapids podcast, former USMNT defender Heath Pearce joins the crew to break down Mauricio Pochettino’s newly finalized 26-man roster, with Tanner Tessmann and Diego Luna off of it. 
The guys also deliver their early 2026 World Cup predictions, discuss Lionel Messi's injury, and debate if KSE stands as the greatest ownership group in sports history. 
Join Andre Simone and Yahir Vazquez to talk all things Rapids, USMNT and beyond.

videos.summary_source_text:
Google's May AI Update Just Changed SEO Forever Want to make money and save time with AI? Join here: https://www.skool.com/ai-profit-lab-7462/about

Get 200+ Free AI SEO Prompts → https://go.juliangoldie.com/chat-gpt-prompts

Get out SEO link building book here 👉 https://go.juliangoldie.com/opt-in?utm=julian

Google May 2026 Core Update + AI Mode: Why Rankings and Clicks Are Splitting (What to Do Next)

Google’s May 2026 core update launched May 21 and may take up to two weeks to roll out, but it’s drawing extra attention because Google also rebuilt search around AI and reported AI Mode has one billion monthly users. The script explains this creates a “double wave” of SERP disruption where rankings and clicks can move independently due to AI overviews and AI Mode, and warns that early “winners/losers” claims are unconfirmed until the rollout settles around June 5 or later. It reiterates Google’s guidance that core updates don’t target specific sites or imply wrongdoing, but recalibrate quality evaluation across the index. The episode outlines a shift from optimizing for clicks to being cited by AI systems, recommends publishing across multiple platforms, strengthening brand authority and mentions, adding schema, and analyzing Search Console by comparing 90 days pre–May 20 vs from June 5, while avoiding changes during rollout.

videos.title: Why early extension ruins contact #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
Why early extension ruins contact #golfswing #golftips Early extension makes it difficult to create space and return the club to the ball consistently. Practicing from slightly closer to the ball with a simple station encourages the hips to move back and stay out of the way. If you early extend from this setup, contact becomes almost impossible — which helps train the correct movement quickly.
If early extension or poor contact is holding your game back, head to the link in our bio. Our coaching plans give you clear priorities and drills to build space and consistency in your swing.
Subscribe to Me and My Golf for more simple golf tips. Early extension makes it difficult to create space and return the club to the ball consistently. videos.title: A Normal Stress Test Doesn’t Always Mean “Healthy”
videos.summary_source_text:
A Normal Stress Test Doesn’t Always Mean “Healthy” Heart disease often develops quietly over time, long before major symptoms ever appear. Many people assume testing will catch every problem early, but some tests are designed to detect only more advanced stages of blockage. That means plaque buildup can still be progressing even when blood flow appears normal. Waiting until disease becomes severe is very different from identifying risk early and taking action sooner.

To learn more about how to book a consultation with me please check out https://aristotleeducation.com

All of my social media and official links: https://linktr.ee/pradipjamnadasmd

Subscribe to the Channel! https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCOtQHehGWtblMp1gZC8Kq3Q?sub_confirmation=1

Remember that my videos are not a substitute for individualized medical care by a qualified physician. Always consult your physicians before making any diet, nutrition, medication, or lifestyle changes.


About Dr. Pradip Jamnadas, MD, MBBS, FACC, FSCAI, FCCP, FACP

The founder and Chief Medical Officer of Cardiovascular Interventions, P.A. in Orlando Florida where, since 1990,  he has been repeatedly recognized in local publications as a Top Doctor performing thousands of interventional procedures in hospital and out patient settings. As a consultant cardiologist with a large diversified inpatient and outpatient practice he is noted for his passions for teaching and illuminating prevention for cardiovascular disease . He is also a clinical assistant professor of medicine at The Florida State University and University of Central Florida. videos.title: Alex Smalley reflects on MAJOR breakthrough & results of Matt Every's U.S. Open qualifier | The Drop
videos.summary_source_text:
Alex Smalley reflects on MAJOR breakthrough & results of Matt Every's U.S. Open qualifier | The Drop The Drop welcomes Alex Smalley to the show, as the PGA Championship runner-up prepares to make his return to action at this week’s Charles Schwab Challenge. He talks about how he preps for tournaments, his goals for the year and using his finish at the last major to propel his game forward. The next major on the schedule is the U.S. Open, and our co-host Matt Every played in a local qualifier to try and make his way into the national championship at Shinnecock. Our cameras were there to document his round. Someone who has already advanced to final qualifying is University of Virginia senior Ben James, who also sits atop the PGA TOUR University leaderboard and will turn pro imminently. But first, he and his teammates will try to succeed at this week’s NCAA Tournament finals. We visited campus to visit him and his team as their season winds down to its exciting finish.

00:00 Introduction
00:34 Preview of the Charles Schwab Challenge
01:27 Interview with Alex Smalley 
05:28 FedExCup Standings Update
05:46 Matt Every’s attempt to qualify for the U.S. Open

14:45 PGA TOUR U Rankings
15:33 Visit to University of Virginia
18:02 Mindful trailer with Patrick Rodgers
20:18 Outro

videos.title: How Does He Stay On His Feet During This Move?
videos.summary_source_text:
How Does He Stay On His Feet During This Move? The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

videos.title: DeepMind's CTO Explains Their Invisible "AI Watermark"
videos.summary_source_text:
DeepMind's CTO Explains Their Invisible "AI Watermark" Did you know your phone can easily tell you which videos or images or AI?

AI videos are getting so realistic to the point where it's bordering on scary. So I point blank asked the CTO of Google DeepMind at Google I/O this year: What is your company doing about this?

His answer was pretty cutting edge: Deepmind build a system called SynthID, an invisible digital watermark baked right into AI-generated content.

So if you’re ever watching a video and think, "Wait, is this real?", you can literally just use Circle to Search on your phone or drop the content into the Gemini app and ask, “Is this AI?”

It will instantly scan the invisible watermark and tell you the truth. 

Send this to someone who NEEDS to know about this (or tag that one friend who always falls for fake AI videos...)

#AI #Gemini #GoogleDeepMind #genAI #aiart Did you know your phone can easily tell you which videos or images or AI?

His answer was pretty cutting edge: Deepmind build a system called SynthID, an invisible digital watermark baked right into AI-generated content.

So if you’re ever watching a video and think, "Wait, is this real?", you can literally just use Circle to Search on your phone or drop the content into the Gemini app and ask, “Is this AI?”

It will instantly scan the invisible watermark and tell you the truth.

videos.title: Därför stämmer inte 40-40-40 om din pension
videos.summary_source_text:
Därför stämmer inte 40-40-40 om din pension Många går och oroar sig för att bli fattigpensionärer, ofta på grund av rubriker som "40-40-40": att vi jobbar 40 timmar i veckan i 40 år för att leva på 40 procent av lönen. Jag pratade själv om det förr, men det stämmer inte för en vanlig löntagare.

Är du en snittsvensk med en lön kring snittet, som jobbar större delen av livet, kan du räkna med ungefär 60 till 70 procent av din slutlön i pension. Det beror på att över en femtedel av din lön redan sätts av automatiskt: den allmänna pensionen (inkomst- och premiepension) plus tjänstepensionen om du har kollektivavtal.

Min poäng är att de flesta inte behöver oroa sig. Extra sparande handlar om guldkant i vardagen som äldre, inte om överlevnad.

Mer om pension, tjänstepension och sparande finns på [rikatillsammans.se](http://rikatillsammans.se) och i RikaTillsammans-podden.

Jag pratade själv om det förr, men det stämmer inte för en vanlig löntagare.

videos.title: Copy This Fleetwood Finish #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
Copy This Fleetwood Finish #golfswing #golftips If your iron shots are inconsistent — thin one swing and fat the next — your arm connection and posture could be the reason. In this lesson, Dan was losing up to 4 inches of height and letting his arms splay out, creating a “chicken wing” and leading to poor contact. We used a simple drill to shorten his through swing and keep the arms closer and more extended — just like Tommy Fleetwood. The result? Better compression, a more stable posture, and pure strikes.
Follow for more practical golf tips from Me and My Golf. If your iron shots are inconsistent — thin one swing and fat the next — your arm connection and posture could be the reason. videos.title: A Tiny Pinch of Salt Before Bed?  Here’s Why Some People Do It | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
A Tiny Pinch of Salt Before Bed?  Here’s Why Some People Do It | Dr. Mandell Could a tiny pinch of mineral salt before bed actually help some people sleep better? In this video, Dr. Mandell explores why certain individuals use a small pinch of Himalayan or natural sea salt at night and the possible physiology behind it. We discuss hydration balance, electrolytes, sodium’s role in nerve signaling and muscle relaxation, adrenal stress, nighttime awakenings, leg cramps, and why low mineral balance may affect how your body feels at rest. This is not a recommendation for everyone, especially if you have high blood pressure, kidney disease, or are on sodium-restricted diets, but understanding the physiology may surprise you. Sometimes the smallest habits can have the biggest effects.

*********************************

Please share this video with your friends and family.  Wishing you and your family many Blessings!  ❤️😊🙏 Dr M

videos.title: How To Boost Hockey Instinct
videos.summary_source_text:
How To Boost Hockey Instinct When you watch McDavid or MacKinnon, it looks like magic which is exactly why most players never try to replicate it.
But it's not magic. It's mechanics. And mechanics can be broken down into learnable pieces, practiced until they're automatic, and turned into instinct.
That's the entire idea behind Train 2.0. My job is to break the movement down. Your job is to put in the reps. And because most of these pieces aren't being taught anywhere else, the work you put in now becomes your advantage on the ice.

videos.title: Canes Take Game 3 and Avs Look to Avoid Being Swept ft. Jonny Lazarus & Sergio Momesso | The Sheet
videos.summary_source_text:
Canes Take Game 3 and Avs Look to Avoid Being Swept ft. Jonny Lazarus & Sergio Momesso | The Sheet The Sheet rolls on as Jonny Lazarus joins the show to break down another thrilling night in the Stanley Cup Playoffs after the Carolina Hurricanes took down the Montreal Canadiens in overtime in Game 3 to grab a 2-1 series lead in the Eastern Conference Finals. Jeff Marek and Jonny dig into the biggest moments from the game, what changed in overtime, the impact of Carolina's depth, and where the Canadiens stand heading into a crucial Game 4. Then the focus shifts west as the Colorado Avalanche stare down elimination against the Vegas Golden Knights and look to keep their season alive tonight. Can Nathan MacKinnon and company respond under pressure, or are the Golden Knights one win away from punching their ticket to the Stanley Cup Final? Later, Sergio Momesso joins the show for an in-depth breakdown of the Hurricanes and Canadiens series, discussing Montreal's playoff run, key matchups, adjustments, and what to expect moving forward. Don't forget to like, comment, and subscribe for more NHL playoff coverage all postseason long.

videos.title: GPT 6 Explained: Release Date, Features & The AGI Questionn
videos.summary_source_text:
GPT 6 Explained: Release Date, Features & The AGI Questionn Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Every other day someone's screaming GPT-6 is dropping next month and it's basically AGI. So I dug into every credible source — OpenAI blogs, Sam Altman interviews, Stargate infrastructure data, the latest benchmarks — to figure out what's actually real. The answer is more interesting than the rumors.

In this video, I break down what's officially confirmed about GPT-6, what's credibly rumored, how it stacks up against GPT-5.5, the realistic release timeline, and the question everyone's asking: is GPT-6 going to be AGI? I give you a probability estimate with actual evidence — not hot takes.

🔔 Subscribe for no-hype AI breakdowns: [your channel link]

📌 CHAPTERS

00:00 The GPT-6 hype problem
01:27 What We Actually Know
02:30 The "Extra Goblins" Comment That Started It All
03:55 The Agentic Leap
05:01 The Real Reason We Can Guess at Timing — Stargate
06:26 GPT-6 vs GPT-5.5: The Honest Comparison
08:30 Who Actually Gets Hit by This
10:30 So Is GPT-6 AGI? Let's Define the Question
11:39 The Benchmark That Should End the Debate
13:17 So What's My Actual Probability Estimate?
14:35 The Punchline: Powerful, Not Magical
15:34 The Risks Nobody's Talking About Yet

🎯 KEY TAKEAWAYS

- GPT-6 has NOT been officially announced — latest shipped model is GPT-5.5 ("Spud"), released April 23, 2026
- Persistent memory + stronger agentic capabilities are the rumored headline features
- Realistic launch window: late 2026 to 2027, gated by Stargate buildout
- Training cost estimates: $10–20 billion (per Dario Amodei and other analysts)
- Probability GPT-6 qualifies as AGI: realistically 1–10%, centered around 5%

📊 SOURCES MENTIONED

- OpenAI Stargate announcement (July 2025)
- Sam Altman CMSWire interview (Dec 2025)
- TechRadar "extra goblins" analysis (April 2026)
- ARC-AGI-3 benchmark (March 2026)
- Yann LeCun commentary on LLM scaling
- McKinsey generative AI economic impact report

💬 QUESTION FOR YOU
What feature are you most excited or most skeptical about for GPT-6? Drop it in the comments — best ones shape future videos on this channel.

videos.title: Claude Mythos: The AI Anthropic Built — Then Locked Away
videos.summary_source_text:
Claude Mythos: The AI Anthropic Built — Then Locked Away Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Everyone is panicking about Claude Mythos — Anthropic's new top-tier model that's so capable they decided the public can't have it. But most of the takes flying around your timeline are from people who never opened the 244-page risk report. I did. And the real story is way more interesting than "scary AI."

In this video I break down what Claude Mythos actually is, why it's locked behind a program called Project Glasswing, what it can do that GPT-4o can't, and whether the AGI panic is justified or just more hype. We get into the emergent zero-day discovery capability that has cybersecurity researchers nervous, the 27-year-old OpenBSD bug Mythos found that nobody else caught, and the leaked Fortune report that forced Anthropic to go public weeks earlier than planned.

Whether you build with these models, write about them, or just want to understand where frontier AI is actually heading in 2026, this is the breakdown to watch.

⏱️ CHAPTERS

00:00 The Mythos panic everyone's getting wrong
01:07 The leak and the reveal
02:38 What Mythos can actually do
05:20 Why the public is locked out
08:24 Is this AGI? Mythos vs. GPT-4 and GPT-4o
10:55 Timeline of how we got here
12:31 What happens next

🔗 SOURCES & FURTHER READING

Anthropic Project Glasswing announcement
Fortune leak report (March 2026)
Claude Mythos System Card (244 pages)
Google Cloud Vertex AI preview confirmation
NBC News cybersecurity coverage
Axios system card review
Gary Marcus commentary

💬 THE QUESTION FOR YOU

Is "defenders first" the right call, or is locking up the most capable model on the planet just a new kind of hype? Drop your take in the comments.

🔔 SUBSCRIBE to BitBiased AI for honest breakdowns of every major frontier model release — Anthropic, OpenAI, Google DeepMind, Meta, xAI. 
New videos weekly.

📺 RELATED VIDEOS ON THIS CHANNEL
https://youtu.be/t8SSyb_Scp8
https://youtu.be/Rkh2kHaaoOg

#ClaudeMythos #Anthropic #AINews #AISafety #Cybersecurity #claude Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Everyone is panicking about Claude Mythos — Anthropic's new top-tier model that's so capable they decided the public can't have it. But most of the takes flying around your timeline are from people who never opened the 244-page risk report. I did. And the real story is way more interesting than "scary AI."

In this video I break down what Claude Mythos actually is, why it's locked behind a program called Project Glasswing, what it can do that GPT-4o can't, and whether the AGI panic is justified or just more hype. We get into the emergent zero-day discovery capability that has cybersecurity researchers nervous, the 27-year-old OpenBSD bug Mythos found that nobody else caught, and the leaked Fortune report that forced Anthropic to go public weeks earlier than planned.

Whether you build with these models, write about them, or just want to understand where frontier AI is actually heading in 2026, this is the breakdown to watch.

⏱️ CHAPTERS

00:00 The Mythos panic everyone's getting wrong
01:07 The leak and the reveal
02:38 What Mythos can actually do
05:20 Why the public is locked out
08:24 Is this AGI? Mythos vs. GPT-4 and GPT-4o
10:55 Timeline of how we got here
12:31 What happens next

🔗 SOURCES & FURTHER READING

Fortune leak report (March 2026)
Claude Mythos System Card (244 pages)
Google Cloud Vertex AI preview confirmation
NBC News cybersecurity coverage

videos.title: Canes Win Another Thriller + a Huge Offseason Ahead for the Devils | Daily Faceoff Live
videos.summary_source_text:
Canes Win Another Thriller + a Huge Offseason Ahead for the Devils | Daily Faceoff Live The Carolina Hurricanes have taken control of the Eastern Conference Final after another dramatic overtime win against the Montreal Canadiens. Andrei Svechnikov delivers the Game 3 winner as Carolina grabs a pivotal 2-1 series lead, but did Montreal miss a massive opportunity to swing the series back in their favour?

We also break down the growing pressure surrounding the Colorado Avalanche after falling behind in their series with Vegas. Despite controlling large stretches of play and owning the expected goals battle in two of three games, the Avs are now on the brink. Is this just bad luck, or are major changes coming if this ends early?

Plus, we continue our NHL offseason preview series with a look at the New Jersey Devils and the huge decisions facing new GM Sunny Mehta in his first summer running the team. Will New Jersey make bold changes to shake up the core, or stay patient with a group that still believes it can contend?

Jeff Marek also joins to discuss whether Vegas’ success after a late coaching change could spark a league-wide trend — and whether Colorado risks overreacting if this season ends in disappointment.

videos.title: The new Ferrari Luce: My HONEST take...
videos.summary_source_text:
The new Ferrari Luce: My HONEST take... This is the Ferrari Luce, Ferrari’s very first all-electric car - and it might be the most controversial car of the decade! 

Explore new and used car deals with Carwow: https://shorturl.at/M75mL

It’s not the first time Ferrari has looked to use electrical power in its cars. The likes of the SF90 and 296 are some of the most popular Ferraris around. But this is the very first all-electric car to roll off the production line at Maranello - and the reception has been… Interesting! 

So what’s all the fuss about? Let’s take a closer look.

In terms of the design, there’s one BIG factor in why people aren’t happy with the looks - and that’s because it wasn’t even designed by Ferrari! It was designed by LoveFrom, a creative collective headed up by ex-Apple designer Sir Jony Ive, and it seems their end product has proven especially divisive. There are ‘floating’ front and rear spoilers, rear-hinged doors for the back seat passengers, and it comes in as Ferrari’s largest car to date - over 5m long! All in all, we worry that it looks more like an indistinguishable Chinese EV, rather than a car worth a £400,000+ price tag! 

Thankfully, there’s a lot to like about the interior. Despite being an EV, physical dials and buttons have been retained. There’s a large, movable infotainment screen in the centre of the dash, but otherwise it’s kept relatively minimalist and clean - unlike most modern cars with fussy, complicated interiors. What’s more, it’s also the second Ferrari to come with 4 doors, and the first to come with a bench seat for 5 passengers in the rear! 

In terms of performance, the Luce is an absolute powerhouse. It comes with four electric motors, and these combine to propel you to 60mph in just 2.5 seconds. It has a top speed of 193mph, and it produces an eye-watering 1,050hp! However, despite its huge size, it’s not the heaviest EV out there, tipping the scales at 2,260kg. That’s 400kg lighter than a BMW iX M70! 

So what do you think - is it worth the huge £474,000 price tag?! And more importantly, do you think Ferrari will actually manage to sell any other?! Let us know in the comments! videos.title: How to Put Like a Pro | Padraig Harrington
videos.summary_source_text:
How to Put Like a Pro | Padraig Harrington As part of my YouTube series  “Ten lessons every golfer should know”, the fifth Paddy’s Golf Tip is on putting, which is in two parts. 
 
In the first instalment,  I explain the basic fundamentals of putting, covering the set up and the stroke. I demonstrate both aspects and explain why a short follow through encourages you to rap your putts.

I also explain why it is important to train your eyes to watch your putts and also provide a cure for the dreaded yips!

#PadraigHarrington #golfing #golftips #paddysgolftips #golf #feedback #putting

SUBSCRIBE FOR MORE CONTENT: https://bit.ly/4gKoVme

About Padraig Harrington: 

Born in Dublin, Padraig is a proud family man married to Caroline with two children, Patrick and Ciaran. As a three time major champion, Padraig is one of the world’s top golfers and his playing record speaks for itself. Following a very successful amateur career, including winning the Walker Cup in 1995 at Royal Porthcawl in one of his three appearances, he turned professional in September 1995.

videos.title: I Found The Secret To Straight Drives
videos.summary_source_text:
I Found The Secret To Straight Drives Have you been struggling to hit the driver as straight and long as you want? In this video, I’m sharing the ultimate driver takeaway secret that I’ve given to my students for years, but recently started using myself to hit straight drives and control my club path.

If you are an everyday golfer who struggles with an inside takeaway, casting, or a wild club face, this simple golf swing visual cue will completely change your game. By using your toe line to marry up your hand line, you can fix your backswing theme and master your downswing delivery. Stop wasting distance with massive curvature—learn how to get your club path to zero for pure, straight tee shots every single time!

Chapters:
00:00 - The Driver Secret I've Kept From Myself
00:43 - Why Amateur Takeaways Fail (The Matchup Trap)
01:21 - How Club Path Controls Distance and Curvature
01:45 - The Perfect Setup: Checking Your Hand Distance
02:37 - The Toe Line Trick: Locking In Your Takeaway
03:31 - How to Use the Toe Line on Your Downswing
04:14 - Putting the Feel Into a Real Shot (Zero Path)

videos.title: OVETIME THRILLER! Andrei Svechnikov's OT Heroics Gives the Carolina Hurricanes a 2-1 Series Lead!
videos.summary_source_text:
OVETIME THRILLER! Andrei Svechnikov's OT Heroics Gives the Carolina Hurricanes a 2-1 Series Lead! The Carolina Hurricanes take a 2-1 series lead after winning ANOTHER game 3-2 in overtime. This time, it was Andrei Svechnikov with the overtime heroics for his second goal of the playoffs. What stood out in a wild Game 3 that saw goals taken off the board and multiple questionable penalty calls and non-calls? Also, how does this win give the Hurricanes all the momentum in the series heading into Wednesday night's Game 4? On the Tuesday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on the Hurricanes' Game 3 overtime win, their second consecutive 3-2 win in bonus hockey, and their fifth such win this postseason. What about winning 3-2 in overtime is the Hurricanes' mo to get the wins? How clutch was Svechnikov in the win? Secondly, what else stood out in the win? How big was it to get goals by Shayne Gostisbehere and Taylor Hall to give the Hurricanes their goals by committee when they needed it the most? Finally, Zach concludes the show by giving his thoughts on how this game gives the Hurricanes all the confidence to head into Game 4 with a chance to take a 3-1 series lead. With them having their first Eastern Conference Final lead since 2006, is there some mojo cooking for the Hurricanes, finally, in the Rod Brind'Amour era in the ECF? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

👋 WELCOME — I’M RICK SHIELS

This channel is all about helping you play better golf and enjoy the game more.
I make videos for every type of golfer — beginners, weekend players, improving golfers and low handicaps chasing new personal bests.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers
• Irons
• Putters
• Wedges
• Golf balls
• Launch monitors
• Golf gadgets
• Budget vs premium clubs

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

If you’re trying to find the best golf driver, best irons, most forgiving clubs or value golf equipment — you’re in the right place.

⭐ BREAK 75 & GOLF ENTERTAINMENT

Break 75 has become one of YouTube’s biggest golf series.
You’ll see:

• Full 18-hole course vlogs
• Course management strategies
• Matches against tour players and creators
• Pressure moments, birdie runs and disaster holes
• Some of the world’s best golf courses
• Real, relatable golf

I also play matches and challenges with some of the biggest names in golf, including:

• Bryson DeChambeau
• Peter Finch
• Good Good
• GM Golf
• Bob Does Sports
• BustaJack
• Grant Horvat
• Me and My Golf
• Danny Maude

These help bring you the most entertaining and competitive golf content possible.

⭐ TRAVEL, COURSE VLOGS & LIV CONTENT

I film at some of the most incredible golf destinations around the world, including LIV tournament venues.
Expect:

• Amazing golf courses
• Behind-the-scenes access
• Player interactions
• Travel insights
• Bucket-list destinations
• Worldwide golf adventures

⭐ THE GOAL

To make golf more fun, more accessible and help you become a better, more confident, happier golfer — whether you’re breaking 100 or trying to break par. In this video Rick Shiels is in Florida, USA at Streamsong. Could this be one of the best golf resorts on the planet?

videos.title: Patrick Rodgers on redefining winning | Mindful
videos.summary_source_text:
Patrick Rodgers on redefining winning | Mindful Patrick Rodgers is one of the most accomplished college players of all-time. The Stanford prodigy won 11 times, tying Tiger Woods’ collegiate record. Despite his early success, the 33-year-old has not earned any wins in over 300 PGA TOUR starts.
 
In December 2025, Rodgers wrote a letter on his journey and how he’s found peace through adversity with his family. The letter explains the veteran’s story in his own words. 11 years later, Rodgers’ pursuit for a win continues, but he’s won much more in life than just a trophy.

SUBSCRIBE TO THE PGA TOUR: https://www.youtube.com/@UCKwGZZMrhNYKzucCtTPY2Nw

The Stanford prodigy won 11 times, tying Tiger Woods’ collegiate record. Despite his early success, the 33-year-old has not earned any wins in over 300 PGA TOUR starts.
 
In December 2025, Rodgers wrote a letter on his journey and how he’s found peace through adversity with his family. The letter explains the veteran’s story in his own words. 11 years later, Rodgers’ pursuit for a win continues, but he’s won much more in life than just a trophy.

👋 WELCOME — I’M RICK SHIELS

This channel is all about helping you play better golf and enjoy the game more.
I make videos for every type of golfer — beginners, weekend players, improving golfers and low handicaps chasing new personal bests.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers
• Irons
• Putters
• Wedges
• Golf balls
• Launch monitors
• Golf gadgets
• Budget vs premium clubs

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

videos.title: I test the BRAND NEW Titleist GTS drivers 👀 #titleist
videos.summary_source_text:
I test the BRAND NEW Titleist GTS drivers 👀 #titleist Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

videos.summary_source_text:
AI som skapar verkligt värde Vad krävs för att lyckas med AI i offentlig sektor – i praktiken? Ta del av inspelningen från Morgondagens Samhälle 2026 där PwC:s experter Charlotte Arnell och Ola Johnsson delar konkreta insikter om hur organisationer kan arbeta strukturerat med styrning, juridik och implementation för att få effekt av sina AI-satsningar.

Du får exempel på vanliga utmaningar, vad som krävs för att komma vidare – och hur AI kan omsättas från idé till faktisk verksamhetsnytta.

Läs mer om offentlig sektor:  https://www.pwc.se/sv/branscher/offentlig-sektor.html

videos.title: 25/05/26 - Ep 11 | The War of Attention: How Social Media & Smart Money Manipulate Retail Traders
videos.summary_source_text:
25/05/26 - Ep 11 | The War of Attention: How Social Media & Smart Money Manipulate Retail Traders In Episode 11 of RIFT Before You Quit, we uncover one of the most important realities of modern financial markets — the War of Attention.

Today’s markets are no longer driven only by charts, fundamentals, or economics. They are increasingly driven by social media narratives, influencer psychology, emotional volatility, and engineered hype cycles.

This session explores how attention itself has become a financial weapon.

We break down how platforms like TikTok, X (Twitter), YouTube, and viral communities can rapidly move markets, create FOMO, and influence millions of retail traders within minutes. From meme coin explosions to influencer-driven volatility, this episode explains why so many retail participants end up becoming liquidity for larger players and institutional smart money.

Inside this episode, we cover:

The rise of the attention economy in financial markets
How influencers and viral narratives create volatility
Why retail traders often enter too late
The psychology behind FOMO and herd behaviour
How smart money quietly accumulates and distributes positions
Meme coin cycles, Dogecoin mania, Trump coin volatility & social hype
Why emotional trading becomes liquidity for institutions
The difference between retail emotion vs institutional structure
How professional systems filter market noise
Why structured execution matters more than ever
How RIFT is designed around disciplined participation and reduced emotional exposure
The future of AI-generated narratives and social trading ecosystems

This episode also explains why markets today move through visibility and attention, not just value. We explore how social reinforcement, viral profit screenshots, and online hype influence retail psychology and create dangerous emotional decision-making environments.

Most importantly, we explain why long-term survival in markets comes from:

Structure
Discipline
Risk management
Consistency
Intelligent execution systems

—not emotional reactions to hype.

As discussed throughout the session, institutions operate through:

Portfolio balancing
Liquidity analysis
Structured allocation
Calm execution systems

while retail traders often operate through:

Panic
FOMO
Impulsive entries
Social pressure
Emotional volatility

This episode is a powerful mindset shift for anyone serious about understanding:

Modern market behaviour
Retail psychology
Institutional positioning
Smart money flow
The future of financial participation

We also discuss how RIFT’s infrastructure focuses on:

Structured execution
Flow-based participation
Intelligent systems
Reduced emotional interaction
Stable capital environments

because in today’s market:

Attention creates volatility.
Emotion creates liquidity.
But structure creates survival.

If you are part of the RIFT ecosystem, a trader, community builder, affiliate, or someone wanting to better understand how modern markets truly operate behind the scenes — this episode is essential viewing.

📌 Next Episode Preview (Episode 12):
The Collapse Cycle – Why Most Traders Eventually Blow Up Their Capital
Topics include:

Leverage destruction
Overexposure mechanics
Compounding drawdowns
Emotional sizing
Liquidation psychology
Survival mathematics
Why aggressive trading eventually collapses

Stay connected with the community and continue learning how institutional structure, AI infrastructure, and disciplined execution are reshaping the future of finance through RIFT. Liquidity analysis
Structured allocation
Calm execution systems

while retail traders often operate through:

Panic
FOMO
Impulsive entries
Social pressure
Emotional volatility

This episode is a powerful mindset shift for anyone serious about understanding:

Modern market behaviour
Retail psychology
Institutional positioning
Smart money flow
The future of financial participation

We also discuss how RIFT’s infrastructure focuses on:

Structured execution
Flow-based participation
Intelligent systems
Reduced emotional interaction
Stable capital environments

because in today’s market:

Attention creates volatility.
Emotion creates liquidity.
But structure creates survival.

If you are part of the RIFT ecosystem, a trader, community builder, affiliate, or someone wanting to better understand how modern markets truly operate behind the scenes — this episode is essential viewing.

📌 Next Episode Preview (Episode 12):
The Collapse Cycle – Why Most Traders Eventually Blow Up Their Capital
Topics include:

Leverage destruction
Overexposure mechanics
Compounding drawdowns
Emotional sizing
Liquidation psychology
Survival mathematics
Why aggressive trading eventually collapses

Stay connected with the community and continue learning how institutional structure, AI infrastructure, and disciplined execution are reshaping the future of finance through RIFT.

videos.title: Hindi | 25th May, Monday - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
Hindi | 25th May, Monday - RIFT Presentation 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

⚠️ Disclaimer: Trading involves risk. Always understand fees, leverage, and market conditions before trading. 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

videos.title: Can the Colorado Avalanche Defy the Odds and Find a Way to Comeback Against Vegas?
videos.summary_source_text:
Can the Colorado Avalanche Defy the Odds and Find a Way to Comeback Against Vegas? Colorado Avalanche face playoff extinction—can Nathan MacKinnon’s return spark an unprecedented comeback against the Vegas Golden Knights? Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson dissect the Avs’ daunting 3-0 series deficit, calling out Vegas’ clinical finishing and the Avalanche’s struggle to capitalize. With MacKinnon’s injury status uncertain and key players like Cale Makar and Valeri Nichushkin in question, the odds look grim, but hope lingers: is a flawless four-game win streak really impossible for this star-studded lineup?

The hosts assess head coach Jared Bednar’s strategy, locker room mentality, and fan reaction, highlighting how every period now feels like overtime. Plus, rumors swirl about GM Chris MacFarland’s potential departure to the Nashville Predators—could front office upheaval add more uncertainty to the Avalanche’s future? Get a passionate breakdown of the Avs’ playoff identity, injury impact, and the high-stakes decisions looming both on and off the ice.

videos.title: Google Bans Coding With Its Biggest Attack On Devs Ever: AntiGravity 2.0
videos.summary_source_text:
Google Bans Coding With Its Biggest Attack On Devs Ever: AntiGravity 2.0 Google just turned Antigravity from a normal AI code editor into a full agent control tower. Developers woke up to a forced update that changed the old workflow, broke setups, and pushed them into a new world where AI agents build, test, and manage code in parallel. With Gemini 3.5 Flash powering the system, Google may have just forced software development into the agent era.

📩 Brand Deals & Partnerships: collabs@nouralabs.com
✉️ General Inquiries: airevolutionofficial@gmail.com
🚀 New Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@space.revolution

📌 What You’ll See:
TechCrunch report on Google launching Antigravity 2.0 at I/O 2026
SOURCE: https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/19/google-launches-antigravity-2-0-with-an-updated-desktop-app-and-cli-tool-at-io-2026/
TechRadar coverage on Gemini CLI users being moved to Antigravity 2.0
SOURCE: https://www.techradar.com/pro/google-is-making-gemini-cli-users-switch-to-its-new-antigravity-2-0-so-what-will-it-mean-for-you
TechCrunch report on Gemini 3.5 Flash and Google’s agentic AI push
SOURCE: https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/19/with-gemini-3-5-flash-google-bets-its-next-ai-wave-on-agents-not-chatbots/
Android Central coverage on Gemini 3.5 Flash powering useful AI agents
SOURCE: https://www.androidcentral.com/apps-software/google-thinks-gemini-3-5-flash-can-finally-make-ai-agents-more-useful
Agentpedia guide on the Gemini CLI to Antigravity CLI migration deadline
SOURCE: https://agentpedia.codes/blog/gemini-cli-to-antigravity-cli-migration

🚨 Why It Matters
This is bigger than one coding tool. Google is pushing developers from writing code directly into supervising AI agents that build in the background. Antigravity 2.0 shows where software development may be heading next, but the messy rollout also shows why many developers feel this future was forced on them before they were ready.

#google #aiagents #antigravity Google just turned Antigravity from a normal AI code editor into a full agent control tower. Developers woke up to a forced update that changed the old workflow, broke setups, and pushed them into a new world where AI agents build, test, and manage code in parallel. With Gemini 3.5 Flash powering the system, Google may have just forced software development into the agent era.

This is bigger than one coding tool. Google is pushing developers from writing code directly into supervising AI agents that build in the background. Antigravity 2.0 shows where software development may be heading next, but the messy rollout also shows why many developers feel this future was forced on them before they were ready.

videos.title: How To Change Directions Backwards
videos.summary_source_text:
How To Change Directions Backwards The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

👉  Drills, breakdowns, progressions, and full mechanics playbook (100's of videos) all available in Train 2.0+ here: https://app.train2point0.com/bundles/membership-plus

videos.title: GPT-5.5 vs Claude 4.7: The $20 AI That Lies Twice as Much (2026)
videos.summary_source_text:
GPT-5.5 vs Claude 4.7: The $20 AI That Lies Twice as Much (2026) Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
I spent 3 months running ChatGPT Plus (GPT-5.5) and Claude Pro (Claude 4.7) side by side on the exact same prompts — and the gap in one specific area genuinely shocked me. In this honest comparison, I break down speed, hallucination rates, coding ability, hidden API costs, vision, ecosystem, and privacy so you know exactly which $20/month AI deserves your money in 2026.

Spoiler: one of these models hallucinates more than TWICE as often as the other — and it's not the one you'd expect.


⏱️ TIMESTAMPS

00:00 The $20 mistake most people are making
01:12 Models & Plans
02:35 Inputs, Outputs & Multimodality
04:17 Context Windows
05:43 Hallucinations & Accuracy
07:50 Coding & Reasoning Performance
09:57 Pricing & Token Efficiency
11:45 Ecosystem, Plugins & Features
13:30 Privacy & Data Retention
14:31 When to Use Which


🔑 KEY FINDINGS
GPT-5.5 hallucinates ~86% of the time when it doesn't know vs Claude's 36%
Claude 4.7 scores ~89% on SWE-Bench Verified vs GPT-5.5's ~84%
GPT-5.5 wins Terminal-Bench: 82.7% vs Claude's 69.4%
GPT-5.5 uses up to 72% fewer output tokens (huge API cost difference)
Both ship 1M-token context windows; only Claude has persistent file-system memory
Claude 4.7 handles images up to 2,576px per side — 3x its previous limit


💬 Drop a comment: "Claude" or "GPT" — which one are you using and why? I read every single one.

👍 If this saved you from picking the wrong AI, hit like and subscribe — the next deep dive is even bigger.

videos.title: Colorado Collapse - Avs Facing Elimination and Hurricanes Ties Series in OT | DFO Rundown
videos.summary_source_text:
Colorado Collapse - Avs Facing Elimination and Hurricanes Ties Series in OT | DFO Rundown The Colorado Avalanche are officially on the brink. After blowing a 3-0 lead to the Vegas Golden Knights, the Avs now trail the series 3-0 and face massive questions heading into Game 4. 

Jeff Marek and Jason Gregor break down Tomas Hertl’s huge night, Nathan MacKinnon battling through injury, Colorado’s goaltending situation, and whether major offseason changes could be coming if the Avs are eliminated.

Plus, the Eastern Conference Final is now tied after the Carolina Hurricanes survived a tight overtime battle against the Montreal Canadiens. The guys discuss Nikolaj Ehlers’ game-winning heroics, Carolina’s physical response, Lane Hutson being targeted, and whether this series is destined to go the distance.

They also dive into the St. Louis Blues’ offseason outlook, possible roster shakeups around the NHL, Dallas’ future, coaching search drama, and what teams like Colorado and Dallas may need to change to finally get over the hump.

videos.title: STOP Taking This Vitamin D Supplement | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
STOP Taking This Vitamin D Supplement | Dr. Mandell Most people think vitamin D is just vitamin D, but a new scientific analysis suggests there may be a common supplement mistake millions are making without realizing it. In this video, Dr. Mandell explains the important differences between vitamin D2 (ergocalciferol) and vitamin D3 (cholecalciferol), why your body may respond differently to each form, how vitamin D functions more like a hormone than a traditional vitamin, and why absorption, dosage, magnesium status, and proper supplementation matter. Before you take your next vitamin D capsule, check your bottle. This simple step could change how you support your bones, muscles, immune system, and overall health.

PUBMED REFERENCES
Brown EIG, Frost GS, Smith CP, et al. Effect of vitamin D2 supplementation on 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 status: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials. Nutrition Reviews. 2025. PMID: 40973107

Tripkovic L, Lambert H, Hart K, et al. Comparison of vitamin D2 and vitamin D3 supplementation in raising serum 25-hydroxyvitamin D status: a systematic review and meta-analysis. American Journal of Clinical Nutrition. 2012, 95(6):1357-1364. PMID: 22552031

Armas LA, Hollis BW, Heaney RP. Vitamin D2 is much less effective than vitamin D3 in humans. Journal of Clinical Endocrinology and Metabolism. 2004, 89(11):5387-5391. PMID: 15531486

Optional fourth if you want stronger immune angle:

Tripkovic L, Wilson LR, Hart K, et al. Daily supplementation with 15 μg vitamin D2 compared with vitamin D3 to increase wintertime 25-hydroxyvitamin D status in healthy South Asian and white European women. American Journal of Clinical Nutrition. 2017, 106(2):481-490. PMID: 28679555

*********************************

Please share this video with your friends and family.  Wishing you and your family many Blessings!  ❤️😊🙏 Dr M

Before you take your next vitamin D capsule, check your bottle. This simple step could change how you support your bones, muscles, immune system, and overall health.

videos.title: ChatGPT Finance Is Freaking People Out
videos.summary_source_text:
ChatGPT Finance Is Freaking People Out This is getting a lot of controversy...

OpenAI just rolled out a new personal finance experience that lets you link your bank accounts directly to ChatGPT via Plaid. 

The pros are that it allows you to basically have a financial advisor at your fingertips. But the cons is that people are worried about the privacy and safety implications of giving a for-profit company that makes money off ads access to your spending habits, investments, and travel history.

Are the potential financial gains and knowledge worth the risk, or is it a big NO for you?

videos.title: This Shaft Angle Mistake Ruins So Many Drives
videos.summary_source_text:
This Shaft Angle Mistake Ruins So Many Drives ⛳️ Take Your FREE Game Assessment → https://meandmygolf.com/game-plan/?utm_source=YouTube&utm_medium=Description&utm_campaign=25.05.26

📱 Download the Swing Coach App (2-Week Free Trial) → https://go.meandmygolf.com/swingcoach/yt/?utm_source=YouTube&utm_medium=Description&utm_campaign=25.05.26

If your driver shots are low, weak, inconsistent, or difficult to strike well, the problem may start before the swing even begins.
In this lesson, Piers works with Greg, a very relatable golfer who was struggling to get the ball in the air and create any real distance with his driver. Like so many players, the issues in the swing were really a series of compensations, but the first thing that needed changing was much simpler than that.
Greg’s shaft angle at address was too far forward, which was affecting the clubface and contributing to the poor strike patterns, low flight, and lack of distance. Once that setup changed, everything else started to improve.
In this video, you’ll learn:

- how the shaft position at setup can influence your driver ball flight
- the simple check you can make in seconds before you hit a drive
- how better setup can help you launch the ball higher and carry it further

If your driver has never felt quite right, this is a great place to start.

videos.title: Gemini Omni Explained | Any-to-Any AI Video, Conversational Editing & the End of Sora's Moat
videos.summary_source_text:
Gemini Omni Explained | Any-to-Any AI Video, Conversational Editing & the End of Sora's Moat Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Everyone's bouncing between five different AI tools just to make one video — script in one, voice in another, visuals in a third, and somehow it still looks like AI made it. Then Google quietly dropped Gemini Omni at I/O 2026 and made that whole stack feel pointless. No relay race between models. 

No handoffs. One brain that takes text, images, audio, or video in — and gives you a generated video out. In this video, I break down exactly what Gemini Omni Flash is, what it can do right now, how it compares to Sora, GPT-4o, and Claude 4 Opus, and the limitations Google's marketing team would rather you didn't hear about.

🔍 WHAT YOU'LL LEARN:

✅ What Gemini Omni actually is (and why "any-to-any" changes everything)
✅ The conversational editing workflow that's blowing creators' minds
✅ How Omni Flash is built — TPU v8, JAX, and the million-chip cluster
✅ Pricing tiers and the AI Ultra catch nobody's talking about
✅ Why YouTube Shorts Remix is the sleeper feature for creators
✅ The real limitations: missing API, SynthID watermark, lock-in

📊 KEY NUMBERS COVERED:

Released May 19th, 2026 inside Gemini, Google Flow, and YouTube Shorts
1,000,000+ TPU chips in Google's training clusters
$20/month entry point on Google AI Plus
$100/month for the new AI Ultra tier with priority Omni access
60 seconds — the current Sora ceiling Omni now competes against
4 input modalities handled by a single model (text, image, audio, video)
3 months — typical lifespan of any frontier-model moat in 2026

⏱️ CHAPTERS:
00:00 The AI Tool Nightmare Google Just Fixed
01:09 What Gemini Omni Actually Is
02:19 The Conversational Editing Workflow
03:29 How It's Built
04:44 Access, Pricing, and the Catch

08:03 The Real Limitations Nobody's Mentioning
09:44 What Comes Next & What You Should Do

videos.title: You Can’t Hit Driver Because You’re Trying To Do THIS
videos.summary_source_text:
You Can’t Hit Driver Because You’re Trying To Do THIS Most golfers struggle with driver for the same reason… they’re trying to do something in the swing that actually makes good drives almost impossible.

And the frustrating part?
A lot of golfers have been taught that this move is actually correct.

In this video I’ll explain one of the biggest misconceptions in the driver swing, why it hurts your strike, speed and consistency, and what great drivers of the golf ball do differently.

We’ll cover:

• The common driver fault holding golfers back
• Why the “feel” most golfers chase is often wrong
• What actually creates better contact and consistency
• How to improve your driver without overcomplicating your swing
• Simple feels and movements you can try immediately

Sometimes better golf isn’t about doing more… it’s about stopping the wrong thing.

If you enjoy the video, make sure to subscribe for more simple, modern golf instruction designed to help you play better and enjoy the game more.

videos.title: Var inte helkund på banken: så delar du upp ekonomin (2026)
videos.summary_source_text:
Var inte helkund på banken: så delar du upp ekonomin (2026) De flesta svenskar har samma bank som sina föräldrar och stannar kvar längre där än hos sin partner. Problemet är att en bank sällan är bäst på allt, och att vara helkund kostar dig pengar i form av dyrare fonder och sämre villkor.

I den här videon går jag igenom hur jag tänker: behåll storbanken till det den behövs för, alltså lönekonto, räkningar och kanske bolånet. Men plocka resten där det är bäst.

Kreditkortet finns gratis hos nischaktörer med poäng och cashback. Sparandet hör sällan hemma på huvudbanken, som ofta har dyrare fonder och gärna styr dig mot sina egna. Där är en nätmäklare som Avanza eller Nordnet, eller en fondrobot som Lysa eller Opti, nästan alltid bättre.

Tumregeln är enkel: fråga vem som är bäst på just den här saken, och lägg den där.

Mer om indexfonder, sparande och pension finns på rikatillsammans.se och i RikaTillsammans-podden.

videos.title: Flashpoint At Mercedes, Magic Lewis + Franco's Finest | 2026 Canadian GP Review | F1 Nation Podcast
videos.summary_source_text:
Flashpoint At Mercedes, Magic Lewis + Franco's Finest | 2026 Canadian GP Review | F1 Nation Podcast Tom Clarkson is joined by F1TV commentary duo Jolyon Palmer and Alex Jacques to reflect on an eventful Canadian Grand Prix.

Kimi Antonelli capitalised on George Russell’s DNF to claim his fourth win in a row and move 43 points clear of his Mercedes teammate at the top of the World Championship standings. What did the guys make of their wheel-to-wheel battles in Montreal? Will Mercedes ask them to dial it down? And how does this result change the mindset of the two title contenders?  

Behind Antonelli, Lewis Hamilton overcame a tense battle with Max Verstappen to score his best result as a Ferrari driver. Was this Lewis’s best weekend since joining the team? And will he continue to avoid the simulator before races?

Tom, Jolyon and Alex also discuss Franco Colapinto’s best finish in Formula 1 and share their thoughts on McLaren’s decision to start both Lando Norris and Oscar Piastri on intermediate tyres. How much responsibility for that call lies with the drivers? 

0:00 - Would Russell have won?
08:08 - 'They're going to clash at some point'
17'01 - Hamilton's 'best' Ferrari weekend?
22:36 - Can Ferrari win Monaco?
24'39 - Max back on the podium
29:37 - McLaren's mistake
36:45 - Franco on a roll
40:36 - Driver of the Day?

videos.title: 466. Allt är bra, men ändå känns det som att något saknas: vad gör man då?
videos.summary_source_text:
466. Allt är bra, men ändå känns det som att något saknas: vad gör man då? Lagomfällan är läget där alla delar av livet är okej men det ändå känns som att något sakans. Inget är tillräckligt dåligt för att man ska vilja bryta upp, men inget känns heller fantastiskt inspirerande. Många upplever att livet blir lite platt och tråkigt, och paradoxalt nog är det ett av de svåraste lägena att ta sig ur eftersom det finns riktigt driv till en förändring. Allt är ju ok, bra och lagom. 

I det här avsnittet får du träffa Stefan och Zandra, ett par i södra Sverige som hamnat just där: två barn, hus, FIRE inom räckhåll, alla nio områden på Livshjulet en sexa av tio. Bra på pappret, men något saknas. Moa Diseborn fyller på med varför hjärtproblem inte går att lösa med Excel, och varför vägen vidare oftast handlar om små säsongsexperiment på tre månader i taget eller leka den gamla leken “gömma nyckeln” med varmare och kallare.

Engelskan fångar det ganska väl: *good is the enemy of great*. Live-rich visar sig vara lika klurigt som get-rich och stay-rich.

00:03:43 - Stefan, Zandra och Moa: vilka är de

00:08:13 - Var det värt det att vara hemma med barnen

01:22:16 - Sammanfattning: mod att kliva fram och prova nästa kapitel

🌟10 biological signals control how you feel every day, but only 1 matters most for YOUR body. Take the free 2-minute quiz to find out: https://drbrg.co/4nRMxdd

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements.

Medications like Ozempic and Mounjaro are trending everywhere, but they’re not the only way to lose weight.

There’s a completely free method that can help reduce appetite naturally, activate fat-burning hormones, lower insulin levels, and allow your body to tap into stored fat for energy.

Sticking to a consistent fasting schedule can help your body become more efficient at burning fat over time. Many people also report improved energy, focus, and fewer cravings once they adapt to this routine. The key is consistency and doing it correctly so your body can fully benefit from the fat-burning state.

Dr. Eric Berg DC Bio:
Dr. Berg, age 61, is a chiropractor who specializes in Healthy Ketosis & Intermittent Fasting. He is the author of the best-selling book "The Healthy Keto Plan" and is the Director of Dr. Berg Nutritionals. He no longer practices but focuses on health education through social media.

Follow Me on Social Media:
YouTube: https://drbrg.co/drberg

Disclaimer:
Dr. Eric Berg received his Doctor of Chiropractic degree from Palmer College of Chiropractic in 1988. His use of “doctor” or “Dr.” in relation to himself solely refers to that degree. Dr. Berg is a licensed chiropractor in Virginia, California, and Louisiana, but he no longer practices chiropractic in any state and does not see patients so he can focus on educating people as a full-time activity, yet he maintains an active license. This video is for general informational purposes only. It should not be used to self-diagnose and it is not a substitute for a medical exam, cure, treatment, diagnosis, prescription, or recommendation. It does not create a doctor-patient relationship between Dr. Berg and you. You should not make any change in your health regimen or diet before first consulting a physician and obtaining a medical exam, diagnosis, and recommendation. Always seek the advice of a physician or other qualified health provider with any questions you may have regarding a medical condition.

videos.title: Cheap Chinese SUV: Bargain or Total Disaster?
videos.summary_source_text:
Cheap Chinese SUV: Bargain or Total Disaster? This is our in-depth Carwow review of the new Chery Tiggo 7 SUV, testing its price, interior, and plug-in hybrid engine.

Get 20% off Car Vertical using code Carwow: https://bit.ly/Carwow-Car-Vertical

It's an ultra-affordable family SUV hitting the UK market from Chinese brand Chery, and it’s aiming to catch the eye of buyers interested in family-friendly SUVs such as the Nissan Qashqai and Kia Sportage. 

Mat's got his hands on it to see if this high-spec, low-cost crossover is a genuine bargain or if you should stick to the established names. So let's dive in…

Starting with the exterior design, the Tiggo 7 makes quite an impression. Up front, you get an imposing face with a massive grille, sharp LED headlights, and a tall, flat bonnet line. Along the side, there’s plenty of chrome trim around the windows to elevate the look, while around the back, it keeps things trendy with a full-width rear LED light bar. All that being said, though, but we can’t help but notice a lot of inspiration being taken from other, more established brands. For example, the grille and headlights remind us of a Q7, while the chrome styling along the side is reminiscent of a Vauxhall Grandland! 

The inspiration from other brands continues on the inside as well. The dash design seems particularly similar to an old BMW X6, the infotainment screen design is similar to that found in a new BMW X6, and the door panels remind us of a Mercedes! 

However, all that being said, the interior is still very well made. The dashboard and upper door cards are upholstered in soft-touch leatherette, which is incredibly impressive for a car at this price point. You get a clean twin-screen display setup on the dash, though the infotainment menus themselves can be a bit awkward and fiddly to navigate on the move. Passenger space is great with plenty of head and legroom for adults in the back, and you get a generous 565-litre boot - though opting for the plug-in hybrid model cuts into that depth slightly.

In terms of engines, you have a choice between standard petrol or a plug-in hybrid. The entry-level car comes with a 1.6-litre turbo petrol engine that can deliver 147hp. However, the pick-of-the-range (and the car we have in this video), is a 1.5-litre petrol plug-in hybrid, which comes with 204hp and the ability to travel for up to 56 miles on electric power alone!

The Chery Tiggo 7 starts from £24,995, with the range-topping plug-in hybrid coming in at £32,995. There’s no denying that the plug-in hybrid is probably the best buy, given it comes with all the added extras that aren’t included with the entry-level car. But should you choose it over its closest rivals? Stick with Mat to see for yourself!

videos.title: FLYING HIGH! Carolina Hurricanes Even Series After Nikolaj Ehlers' OT Winner in Game 2!
videos.summary_source_text:
FLYING HIGH! Carolina Hurricanes Even Series After Nikolaj Ehlers' OT Winner in Game 2! The Carolina Hurricanes tied the Eastern Conference Final by winning Game 2 Saturday night, 3-2 in overtime! What a game from Nikolaj Ehlers, who was the hero with the overtime game-winning goal. What stood out in the win, and how can the Hurricanes take a 2-1 series lead on Monday night's Game 3? On the Monday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on the Hurricanes' Game 2 win in overtime, which seems to be the norm with three straight wins in overtime in the second game of a series. More importantly, how clutch was Ehlers in the win, scoring two goals to lead the way? Also, Eric Robinson is feeling it with two goals in two games to bring the scoring depth. Secondly, Zach shares his thoughts on how the Hurricanes played a much better Game 2 compared to their Game 1 loss on Thursday night. It was a night-and-day difference for the Hurricanes as they split the first two games at home. Finally, Zach concludes the show by previewing Monday night's Game 3 in Montreal. Now that the series shifts to the Bell Centre, what do the Hurricanes need to do to take a 2-1 series lead? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

videos.title: DRAMATIC Final 9 Holes in Belgium | 2026 Soudal Open
videos.summary_source_text:
DRAMATIC Final 9 Holes in Belgium | 2026 Soudal Open Watch the drama of a thrilling Back 9 On Sunday, presented by Nexo, as Richard Sterne clinched victory in Belgium in dramatic fashion. Starting the day five shots off the lead, Sterne carded a final round of 66 whilst producing one of the shots of his life to secure his seventh DP World Tour title, snatching victory from fellow South African Zander Lombard.

Sterne’s previous DP World Tour victory came at the 2013 Joburg Open, where he held off the likes of Brooks Koepka and Tommy Fleetwood in Johannesburg.
