videos.title: The Hockey World Went Into Overreaction Overload After the Avs Game 1 Loss
videos.summary_source_text:
The Hockey World Went Into Overreaction Overload After the Avs Game 1 Loss Colorado Avalanche’s struggles spark overreactions after Game 1 loss to Vegas Golden Knights. Are Nathan MacKinnon and company really in trouble, or is it just playoff noise? Hosts Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson debate whether execution, not Vegas’ dominance, determined the outcome, and dissect what went wrong for the league’s top-scoring team—including Scott Wedgewood’s performance in net and key absences like Cale Makar.

The episode spotlights how analytics and heat maps may tell a misleading story about the Avalanche’s efforts, and questions whether Vegas’ win was a product of structure or simply capitalizing on Colorado’s mistakes. Listeners get insider takes on playoff overreactions, media narratives, and why Carter Hart’s goaltending gives Vegas confidence. Plus, thoughts from the Subtext Crew, potential returns from injury, and what changes the Avalanche must make heading into Game 2. Can Colorado bounce back and reclaim control of the series?

Hosts Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson debate whether execution, not Vegas’ dominance, determined the outcome, and dissect what went wrong for the league’s top-scoring team—including Scott Wedgewood’s performance in net and key absences like Cale Makar.

The episode spotlights how analytics and heat maps may tell a misleading story about the Avalanche’s efforts, and questions whether Vegas’ win was a product of structure or simply capitalizing on Colorado’s mistakes. Listeners get insider takes on playoff overreactions, media narratives, and why Carter Hart’s goaltending gives Vegas confidence. Plus, thoughts from the Subtext Crew, potential returns from injury, and what changes the Avalanche must make heading into Game 2. Can Colorado bounce back and reclaim control of the series?

videos.title: You Might Be Destroying Healthy Food
videos.summary_source_text:
You Might Be Destroying Healthy Food Fasting is not just about eating less, it is also about how the food is prepared. High heat cooking methods like frying, grilling, and charring can create inflammatory compounds that place extra stress on the body. Gentler cooking methods, like steaming, slow cooking, and moist heat, help preserve the food while reducing that inflammatory load. Sometimes the difference is not only what is eaten, but whether the food was nourished or destroyed before it ever reached the plate.

This is a clip form my video "How to Fast for Different Goals"

videos.title: Boston Dynamics’ New Upgraded ATLAS Just Went BEAST MODE
videos.summary_source_text:
Boston Dynamics’ New Upgraded ATLAS Just Went BEAST MODE Boston Dynamics just revealed a major Atlas upgrade. The new Atlas can lift a loaded mini-fridge, handle shifting weight, and move its body in ways humans physically cannot. At the same time, Unitree’s G1 is responding to live voice commands, while Gatsby is testing humanoid robots as on-demand home cleaners. Humanoid robots are no longer just doing impressive demos. They are starting to move into real work.

📩 Brand Deals & Partnerships: collabs@nouralabs.com
✉️ General Inquiries: airevolutionofficial@gmail.com
🚀 New Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@space.revolution

Boston Dynamics explains how Atlas learned to lift a fridge

This is bigger than another robot demo. Atlas is now being trained for physical work with heavy, awkward objects, shifting weight, and real-world uncertainty. Unitree is pushing humanoids toward voice-controlled actions, while Gatsby is testing a service model where people book robots for home cleaning. Factories, warehouses, and homes may be the first real battlegrounds for humanoid robots.

videos.title: How To Flip The Puck Over Your Opponent's Stick
videos.summary_source_text:
How To Flip The Puck Over Your Opponent's Stick The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

#train2point0 #downhillskating #hockeytraining #NHL The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

👉  Drills, breakdowns, progressions, and full mechanics playbook (100's of videos) all available in Train 2.0+ here: https://app.train2point0.com/bundles/membership-plus

videos.title: If I Could Teach Golfers ONE Thing… It Would Be This
videos.summary_source_text:
If I Could Teach Golfers ONE Thing… It Would Be This https://hackmotion.com/affiliates/alex/?aff=198&utm_source=youtube&utm_medium=affiliate&utm_campaign=alexelliott&utm_content=pp&utm_term=21_05_2026

Most golfers try to compress their irons by hitting down harder… but that’s not what creates a pure strike.

Join my whatsapp channel to recieve tips, drills, PDF guides and so much more https://whatsapp.com/channel/0029Vb8Q6rHG3R3bSfYTJx2q

Most golfers try to compress their irons by hitting down harder… but that’s not what creates a pure strike.

In this video, I’ll show you the real key to compressing your irons consistently the same move I teach golfers of all levels to improve strike, contact, and ball flight fast. 

You’ll learn:

How to properly compress your irons
What actually creates that clean, powerful strike
The biggest mistake stopping golfers from compressing the ball
A simple drill to improve your iron strike instantly
How to create better contact with every club in the bag

If you struggle with thin shots, heavy strikes, weak contact, or inconsistent iron play, this lesson will completely change how you think about impact.

Master this move, and you’ll start striking your irons cleaner, lower, and more powerfully than ever before.

videos.title: Round 1 Highlights | 2026 Soudal Open
videos.summary_source_text:
Round 1 Highlights | 2026 Soudal Open Tune in to all the best moments from Round One of the 2026 Soudal Open at Rinkven International Golf Club in Belgium. The day featured eagle hole-outs, a hole-in-one and plenty of birdies. Leading the way at 8-under is Zander Lombard, with Jacob Skov Olesen and Richard Sterne just one shot back, while 10 players sit tied at 5-under.

► SUBSCRIBE: http://et.golf/Subscribe 

After a break for the US PGA Championship last week, the European Swing resumes with the second of six events across the continent and the first of five consecutive national opens on the DP World Tour schedule as the Tour returns to Belgium for the Soudal Open. While this event can trace its roots back well over 100 years, this week sees the 20th time it has been part of the DP World Tour schedule. First played in 1910 and won by 1907 Open champion Arnaud Massy, it would be played every year until 1958 barring breaks for the two World Wars. Local star Thomas Detry is likely to be the headline act this week as he, almost unbelievably, searches for his first DP World Tour win after five runner-up finishes.

Welcome to the Official YouTube channel of the DP World Tour. Every week you can find extended tournament highlights and individual player highlights from the likes of Rory McIlroy, Justin Rose and Tommy Fleetwood. This is also the home of the 14 Club Challenge, Little Interviews, Fastest Hole and many more challenges, pranks and world record attempts featuring the biggest golfers in the world.

Hur kan 100 % av nordiska aktiefonder underprestera index över 10 år? S&P Dow Jones har precis släppt SPIVA Europe Year-End 2025 — den 12:e rapporten i rad. Slutsatsen är obekvämt konsekvent:

▪ 75 % av aktiva aktiefonder i Europa slog inte sitt index 2025
▪ 85 % av Sverige-fonder underpresterade 2025
▪ 98 % av globalfonder i euro underpresterade på 10 år
▪ 100 % av nordiska aktiefonder underpresterade S&P Nordic BMI på 10 år

Och 2025 var faktiskt en gynnsam marknad för aktiv förvaltning — hög spridning, låg korrelation. Tre av fyra förlorade ändå.

videos.title: How To ACTUALLY Do Lunges (Feat. The “Rock”)
videos.summary_source_text:
How To ACTUALLY Do Lunges (Feat. The “Rock”) If you stopped doing lunges because the balance requirement keeps you from using enough weight to actually build your legs, you’re going to love this fix. Whether you’re doing dumbbell lunges or barbell lunges, the way you step makes all the difference.

The biggest mistake people make on lunges is keeping their feet too narrow. They think every step has to move straight forward or straight back on a tightrope. The problem is, the second you add any meaningful weight, that narrow base of support makes balance almost impossible.

Watch Dwayne “The Rock” Johnson perform walking dumbbell lunges in this clip and pay attention to where his feet land. He doesn’t just step straight ahead. He steps up and slightly out with every rep. By the time he reaches the bottom of the lunge, his feet are several inches wider apart, giving him a much stronger base to push from.

That one adjustment can completely change the exercise.

The dumbbell lunge and barbell lunge are two of the best leg exercises you can do, but too many people abandon them for machines because they feel unstable. That’s a mistake. Lunges give you unilateral leg strength, athletic carryover, glute and quad development, and a benefit you simply don’t get from relying only on squat patterns.

Once you fix your balance, I’d suggest starting with reverse lunges whenever possible, especially if you deal with knee tendonitis or anterior knee pain. Reverse lunges tend to be more knee-friendly than forward lunges because they usually create less forward stress at the knee.

If you do perform forward lunges, don’t slide or reach the foot low into the ground. Step up, then place the foot down from above. As you’ll see in the full video linked to this Short, The Rock does exactly that. This helps reduce shearing stress on the knee while keeping the movement strong and controlled.

Fix the step, widen the base, and suddenly lunges stop feeling like a balance test and start feeling like the powerful leg builder they were always supposed to be.

For more tips on how to build muscle and get bigger legs, be sure to stay tuned to this channel and remember to subscribe so that you never miss another video from a physical therapist with a pro sports background as a PT and strength coach.

For more tips on how to build muscle and get bigger legs, be sure to stay tuned to this channel and remember to subscribe so that you never miss another video from a physical therapist with a pro sports background as a PT and strength coach.

You can subscribe to this channel here - http://bit.ly/2b0coMW

Get Your Workout & Meal Plan:
📝 https://www.athleanx.com

Connect With Me:
👉 https://www.instagram.com/athleanx 

For complete step-by-step workout programs, head on over to athleanx.com and make sure to use the program selector to find the training plan that is best suited to your personal goals.

Jeff Cavaliere MSPT, CSCS served as both the head physical therapist and assistant strength coach for the New York Mets. Jeff earned his Masters of Physical Therapy and Bachelor’s of Physioneurobiology from the College of Health Sciences University of Connecticut Storrs. He is a certified Strength and Conditioning Specialist by the National Strength and Conditioning Association (NSCA).
videos.summary: How To ACTUALLY Do Lunges (Feat. The “Rock”) If you stopped doing lunges because the balance requirement keeps you from using enough weight to actually build your legs, you’re going to love this fix.

videos.title: Is it a Budget Masterpiece or an Outdoor Flop? LM1 Real Review
videos.summary_source_text:
Is it a Budget Masterpiece or an Outdoor Flop? LM1 Real Review The Shot Scope LM1 accuracy test will shock you… Is it a budget masterpiece or an outdoor flop? 

In this video, we put the ultra-affordable, pocket-sized Shot Scope LM1 launch monitor through its paces to find out the absolute truth. No subscription fees, a built-in display, and an amazing price point make it look like a game-changer—but there is a massive catch you need to know before you buy it. 

We threw the LM1 up against the industry gold standard, the GC Quad, in a head-to-head battle both indoors and out on the golf course. The data split was unbelievable. Indoors, this tiny Doppler radar is an absolute genius, matching the premium data within a couple of yards. But once you take it outside into the wind? The accuracy claims face a serious reality check. 

Watch to see the exact numbers, find out how it compares to the FlightScope Mevo Gen 2, SkyTrak, and The Stack System, and learn whether this device will revolutionise your practice net or end up gathering dust in the corner of your room.

videos.title: Har DU känt Mellanförskap: Känslan när man inte hör hemma eller passar in!
videos.summary_source_text:
Har DU känt Mellanförskap: Känslan när man inte hör hemma eller passar in! Har du någonsin känt att du inte riktigt passar in någonstans? 🥺 Så har jag känt nästan hela mitt liv och det finns faktiskt ett ord för det där. Mellanförskap
I den här nya serien ”Filip Tänker Högt” tar jag upp tankar och funderingar i mitt huvud. 

Mellanförskap handlar egentligen om känslan av att stå mellan två världar utan att riktigt känna att man passar in fullt ut i någon av dem och jag tror länge att jag såg det som en svaghet. Men idag ser jag det mer som att jag fått flera perspektiv istället.

Här är lite insikter och sammanfattning från videon:
-Det tog lång tid innan jag förstod att jag inte behövde välja en sida. Jag fick skapa min egen identitet istället.

Det som gjorde att jag kände mig annorlunda när jag var yngre är nog också det som gjort mig till den jag är idag.
Man behöver inte passa perfekt in i ett fack för att höra hemma.

Det är okej att vara lite mitt emellan. Det betyder inte att man är mindre hel.

Jag tror många spenderar mycket energi på att försöka passa in, när det egentligen är våra olikheter som gör oss intressanta.

Man blir väldigt bra på att förstå människor när man själv vet hur det känns att känna sig utanför.

videos.title: The Vitamin D Secret
videos.summary_source_text:
The Vitamin D Secret How can you overcome vitamin D deficiency on the carnivore diet without increasing your intake? Discover how the carnivore diet can improve gut health, insulin resistance, and chronic inflammation to improve vitamin D absorption and help optimize its benefits naturally.

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

🌟 10 biological signals control how you feel every day, but only 1 matters most for YOUR body. Take the free 2-minute quiz to find out: https://drbrg.co/4uWwxZJ

0:00 Introduction: Vitamin D and the carnivore diet 
1:01 Magnesium, zinc, vitamin K2, and D3
1:55 Other barriers to vitamin D absorption
2:11 Carnivore diet benefits 
3:19 Vitamin K2 benefits 
3:57 Cholesterol and vitamin D 
5:01 Vitamin D receptor resistance 
6:14 Carnivore diet results 

Keep watching for more on the carnivore diet and other ways to support gut health:
▶️ https://youtu.be/jZ4jNALF9Ys
▶️ https://youtu.be/zXd86RvNzZA
▶️ https://youtu.be/RCjQAQuWiYc

Many people notice major improvements on the carnivore diet, including reduced symptoms of autoimmune disease, less joint pain, improved mood, and better overall health.

Although beef, chicken, pork, and other meats are relatively low in vitamin D, the carnivore diet can still help correct vitamin D deficiency. Simply taking more vitamin D doesn’t always solve the problem.

Vitamin D depends on several important cofactors, including magnesium, zinc, vitamin K2, retinol, and cholesterol, the raw material used to make vitamin D in the body.

Vitamin D enters your cells through the vitamin D receptor. Vitamin D receptor resistance is very common, often caused by obesity, insulin resistance, and poor gut health.

Meat is a potent source of highly bioavailable nutrients, especially the cofactors needed for vitamin D. Many plant foods contain antinutrients that can interfere with nutrient absorption.
Vitamin D is made from cholesterol, so inadequate cholesterol intake can also impact vitamin D production.

The carnivore diet helps to reduce gut inflammation, increase autophagy, improve bile flow, reduce SIBO, and eliminate candida overgrowth, all of which support better vitamin D absorption and overall health.

How can you overcome vitamin D deficiency on the carnivore diet without increasing your intake? Discover how the carnivore diet can improve gut health, insulin resistance, and chronic inflammation to improve vitamin D absorption and help optimize its benefits naturally.

Many people notice major improvements on the carnivore diet, including reduced symptoms of autoimmune disease, less joint pain, improved mood, and better overall health.

Although beef, chicken, pork, and other meats are relatively low in vitamin D, the carnivore diet can still help correct vitamin D deficiency. Simply taking more vitamin D doesn’t always solve the problem.

Vitamin D depends on several important cofactors, including magnesium, zinc, vitamin K2, retinol, and cholesterol, the raw material used to make vitamin D in the body.

Vitamin D enters your cells through the vitamin D receptor. Vitamin D receptor resistance is very common, often caused by obesity, insulin resistance, and poor gut health.

Meat is a potent source of highly bioavailable nutrients, especially the cofactors needed for vitamin D. Many plant foods contain antinutrients that can interfere with nutrient absorption.
Vitamin D is made from cholesterol, so inadequate cholesterol intake can also impact vitamin D production.

The carnivore diet helps to reduce gut inflammation, increase autophagy, improve bile flow, reduce SIBO, and eliminate candida overgrowth, all of which support better vitamin D absorption and overall health.

This golf lesson covers how open club face position causes early release and flipping and provides clear instruction on how to use wrist angles to add shaft lean and compression for more solid iron contact.

👋 WELCOME — I’M RICK SHIELS

This channel is all about helping you play better golf and enjoy the game more.
I make videos for every type of golfer — beginners, weekend players, improving golfers and low handicaps chasing new personal bests.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers
• Irons
• Putters
• Wedges
• Golf balls
• Launch monitors
• Golf gadgets
• Budget vs premium clubs

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

• Amazing golf courses

• Worldwide golf adventures

videos.title: Are Lamborghinis ACTUALLY FAST? Urus v BMW M5 DRAG RACE
videos.summary_source_text:
Are Lamborghinis ACTUALLY FAST? Urus v BMW M5 DRAG RACE BMW M5 Touring vs Lamborghini Urus super estate vs SUV drag race, rolling race, and brake test with Mat Watson.

Thanks to https://www.instagram.com/seanysgarage for joining us for this race!

Explore new and used car deals with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-14-05

It’s time for an epic hybrid estate vs performance SUV showdown!We’ve got Mat in his new BMW M5 Touring, and it’s going head-to-head against a Lamborghini Urus over the quarter-mile. Will this hefty V8 hybrid have what it takes to bring down the lighter Italian super-SUV? Let’s take a closer look at the performance stats…Starting with the BMW M5, it’s powered by a 4.4-litre twin-turbo V8 coupled with an electric motor. Combined, they produce 727hp and 1,000Nm of torque. All that power is sent to all four wheels via an 8-speed automatic gearbox. It costs £116,160, and it tips the scales at a heavy 2,475kg!

Then we have the Lamborghini. It’s powered by a 4-litre twin-turbo V8, and this puts down 650hp and 850Nm! It comes equipped with all-wheel drive, and it costs £172,500. What’s more, despite being an SUV, it’s a fair bit lighter than the BMW, weighing in at 2,200kg! 

So the BMW is up on power, but will the additional 300kg help the Urus snatch the win? There’s only one way to find out… LET’S RACE!

BMW v Porsche v Audi v AMG drag race: https://bit.ly/Carwow-Drag-Race-14-05 BMW M5 Touring vs Lamborghini Urus super estate vs SUV drag race, rolling race, and brake test with Mat Watson.

Thanks to https://www.instagram.com/seanysgarage for joining us for this race!

It's Eastern Conference Final Go Time for the Carolina Hurricanes ft. Mike Maniscalco It's an Eastern Conference Final Gameday for the Carolina Hurricanes as they take on the Montreal Canadiens in Game 1 on Thursday night. After almost two weeks of waiting, it's go time. Zach is joined by Hurricanes play-by-play and CanesCast co-host Mike Maniscalco to preview Game 1 and the ECF as a whole. videos.title: UK | 20th May, Wed - Ep.9 - The Institutional Playbook: How Smart Money Really Controls the Market
videos.summary_source_text:
UK | 20th May, Wed - Ep.9 - The Institutional Playbook: How Smart Money Really Controls the Market Welcome to Episode 9 of RIFT Before You Quit — one of the most eye-opening and advanced sessions in the entire series so far.

In this episode, we dive deep into the Institutional Playbook and uncover how professional capital, hedge funds, and “smart money” truly operate behind the financial markets.

Most retail traders are taught to chase profits, predict price movements, and react emotionally. But institutions operate completely differently. They focus on:

✅ Capital preservation
✅ Risk distribution
✅ Structured execution
✅ Long-term survivability
✅ Portfolio architecture
✅ Smart liquidity positioning
✅ Consistent compounding

This episode explains why institutional systems survive for decades while most retail traders struggle with volatility, leverage, emotional decision-making, and poor risk management.

You’ll learn how hedge funds structure billion-dollar portfolios, how smart money quietly accumulates positions before major market moves, and how institutions use liquidity sweeps and portfolio balancing to stay ahead of the crowd.

We also explore:

🔹 The difference between retail mindset vs institutional mindset
🔹 How smart money flows through the market
🔹 Why survivability is more important than prediction
🔹 The real purpose of risk management
🔹 Liquidity hunting and stop-loss sweeps
🔹 Dynamic portfolio rotation during different market conditions
🔹 Why institutions prioritize structure over emotion
🔹 How AI, automation, and structured execution are changing modern finance

Most importantly, this episode connects these institutional principles directly to the architecture behind the RIFT Auto Trading Engine — showing how diversification, structured allocation, and disciplined execution are built into the system itself.

This is not hype.
This is a deep educational breakdown of how professional capital behaves in the real world.

If you want to understand how markets truly function behind the scenes — this episode is essential viewing.

🔥 Key Takeaway:
The market does not reward emotion in the long run. It rewards structure, discipline, survivability, and intelligent execution.

📌 Topics Covered In This Episode:

• Institutional Trading Psychology
• Hedge Fund Portfolio Structures
• Smart Money Behavior
• Liquidity Sweeps & Stop Hunts
• Capital Preservation Strategies
• Dynamic Portfolio Rotation
• Retail vs Institutional Decision Making
• AI & Structured Market Participation
• RIFT Auto Trading Engine Concepts
• Long-Term Compounding Principles

🚀 NEXT EPISODE PREVIEW – Episode 10
The Future of Financial Infrastructure

We’ll explore:
⚡ AI-driven finance
⚡ Autonomous capital systems
⚡ On-chain execution infrastructure
⚡ Machine-assisted allocation
⚡ Financial automation trends
⚡ The next evolution beyond traditional trading apps

🟢 Monday
🟢 Wednesday
🟢 Friday

videos.title: Avalanche Mistakes Lead to Golden Knights Goals in Game 1 Loss
videos.summary_source_text:
Avalanche Mistakes Lead to Golden Knights Goals in Game 1 Loss Colorado Avalanche undone by self-inflicted wounds in Western Conference Finals Game 1. Despite a flurry of high-danger chances and 38 shots on net, the Avs fall 4-2 to the Vegas Golden Knights as costly mistakes and missed opportunities haunt their playoff run. Can the Avalanche regroup and find the discipline needed to turn the series in their favor?

Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson break down Carter Hart’s stellar goaltending, Ross Colton’s controversial penalty, and Nathan MacKinnon’s highlight-reel assist that gave the Avalanche life. Discussions spotlight Vegas’ defensive structure, the Avs’ erratic shot accuracy, and the critical absence of Cale Makar. With Brett Kulak’s under-the-radar performance and Valeri Nichushkin’s creative goal, the episode explores what Colorado must adjust ahead of Game 2. Will the Avalanche capitalize on their chances next time, or are the Golden Knights in control of the series momentum?

videos.title: Hindi | 20th May, Wednesday - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
Hindi | 20th May, Wednesday - RIFT Presentation 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

⚠️ Disclaimer: Trading involves risk. Always understand fees, leverage, and market conditions before trading. 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

videos.title: The Problem With Constant Snacking
videos.summary_source_text:
The Problem With Constant Snacking Today’s food supply exposes the body to far more chemicals, additives, and processed ingredients than ever before. One of the simplest ways to lower that toxic load is not constantly eating throughout the day. Cutting out snacks and reducing how often food is consumed can significantly decrease the amount of toxins entering the body on a daily basis. Sometimes the healthiest change is not adding more, but finally giving the body a break.

videos.title: How To Keep Momentum After Contact
videos.summary_source_text:
How To Keep Momentum After Contact The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

videos.summary_source_text:
This Simple Food Transforms Your Body in 5 Days What would happen if you ate only sardines for 5 days? This might sound extreme, but the results can be surprising.

🌟 Which of the 10 health signals are you missing? Take the 2-minute quiz to discover your #1 Health Lever: https://drbrg.co/4dHGVyz

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements.

With zero carbs and zero sugar, your body quickly shifts from burning sugar to burning fat. Within the first day, you begin producing ketones, a powerful fuel source that supports brain function and fat loss.

By the end of the five days, your body becomes more efficient at using fat for fuel, which can help stabilize energy levels throughout the day. Many people also notice reduced cravings and a stronger sense of control over their appetite. This kind of reset can give you insight into how your body responds to simple, nutrient-dense foods.

Dr. Berg, age 61, is a chiropractor who specializes in Healthy Ketosis & Intermittent Fasting. He is the author of the best-selling book "The Healthy Keto Plan" and is the Director of Dr. Berg Nutritionals. He no longer practices but focuses on health education through social media.

Gluten-free! High protein! Protein cheese muffins in 15 minutes! Highly recommended! Gluten-free! High protein! Protein cheese muffins in 15 minutes! Highly recommended!

2 eggs.
3.5 oz (100 g) mozzarella cheese.
3.5 oz (100 g) parmesan cheese.
1/3 cup (80 g) yogurt.
2 tbsp coconut oil.
2 tsp stevia powder.
1 tsp baking powder.
1 cup (110 g) almond flour.
If you like sugar-free recipes, subscribe to Schnelle Rezepte!
Bake in a preheated oven at 360°F (180°C) for about 15–20 minutes.

In this deep dive, I break down every major announcement from Google I/O 2026, from the new Gemini 3.5 Flash and Gemini Omni models to the agent platforms (Spark, Antigravity), the Universal Cart that shops for you, AI Studio's app-generation magic, and the creator tools reshaping Flow and Workspace.

videos.title: Jag tror på forskningen men har en aktiv fond som gått bra
videos.summary_source_text:
Jag tror på forskningen men har en aktiv fond som gått bra Av Europas globalfonder som låg i topp-kvartilen 2021 är 1,22 % kvar i topp-kvartilen 2025 — alltså en fond av åttiotvå. 35 % av topp-fonderna slogs ihop eller lades ned under perioden. Och kanske mest kontraintuitivt: 21 % av fonderna i botten-halvan 2015–2020 klättrade upp till topp-halvan 2020–2025, mer än de 16 % som faktiskt stannade kvar i toppen.

Det är ett av de starkaste argumenten för bred billig indexfond eller fondrobot framför att försöka välja "rätt" aktiv förvaltare.
Källa: S&P Dow Jones Indices, Europe Persistence Scorecard, år-slut 2025 (publicerad maj 2026). Rapporten täcker alla aktivt förvaltade fonder i Europa och rensar för survivorship bias.

videos.title: The BEST Putter Odyssey Has Ever Made?
videos.summary_source_text:
The BEST Putter Odyssey Has Ever Made? Rick Shiels gets his hands on the new Odyssey Damascus Milled Putters, and it's unlike anything Odyssey have released before. From the material to the feel to the price, this putter raises the bar for everyone else — and we break down exactly why.

Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.
Rick Shiels gets his hands on the new Odyssey Damascus Milled Putter Lineup, and it's unlike anything Odyssey have released before. From the material to the feel to the price, this putter raises the bar for everyone else — and we break down exactly why.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

videos.title: VINTAGE: Dmitry Kulikov Selected No. 14 overall in 2009 NHL Draft

• Unlock hip rotation in the golf swing without lead-side feels — trail-side is easier and more effective

Eric Cogorno has coached golfers at every level and consistently finds that activating the trail side is the fastest path to better golf swing mechanics for players over 50. In this video, he breaks down trail leg function using real-time Swing Coach App golf swing analysis so you can see exactly what your lower body is doing — and fix it rep by rep. videos.title: Movement Is a Skill — Not Just Exercise
videos.summary_source_text:
Movement Is a Skill — Not Just Exercise Movement is not only about exercise. It is also a skill.
Just like learning an instrument or a language, the body can learn more efficient, coordinated, and comfortable ways of moving.
In this video, I explore why movement is a skill — and why this changes everything for people who have been trying to force progress through effort alone.

If you’d like to explore this more deeply, I’ve created a free guided somatic session here: https://taro-iwamoto.thinkific.com/courses/masterclass
 
If you’d like personal support, you’re welcome to book a one-on-one Zoom call with me here: https://calendly.com/taroiwamoto/45min

If you feel ready for deeper, more structured support, you can learn more about my course here:
https://taroiwamoto.com/thecourse

I help self-aware adults who are exhausted by recurring pain, tension, and fear cycles despite everything they’ve tried, and who are ready to stop chasing fixes and follow a deeper somatic, nervous-system-based path to lasting change, so they can trust their body again and fully live their life with ease, freedom, and confidence.

videos.summary_source_text:
Did you know this about Tour Players clubs 🤯 Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

To cut through the hype.

To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.
